metadata.budget: RunPod: ~$3 (concurrent test adds ~$0.50 vs v3; no second volume needed). GCP: ~$3-6 (infra already up; 4 models × $0.095 = $0.38 training). Global cap: $6.00. Check cumulative before each new job launch.
metadata.track-a-coordination: Track A (overnight-mambavesselnet.yaml) may be running on test/mambavesselnet. This plan (Track B) runs on qa/gcp-runpod-3rd-pass (same base branch). P2.T0 MUST check Track A state before launching. If Track A is still running: Track B uses SAME volume (minivess-dev) with isolated MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=/opt/vol/mlruns/track-b (subdir isolation, not separate volume). If Track A is done: Track B uses /opt/vol/mlruns directly (default path). NO second volume needed for Track A/B coexistence.

prerequisite-env-vars.for-gcp: PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN, HF_TOKEN, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI (= Cloud Run URL)
prerequisite-env-vars.banned-from-gcp-yaml: DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL, DVC_S3_ACCESS_KEY, DVC_S3_SECRET_KEY, DVC_S3_BUCKET, MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI, MLFLOW_CLOUD_USERNAME, MLFLOW_CLOUD_PASSWORD

done.description: smoke_test_gcp.yaml: GCS data source, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI direct ref
Pulumi stack deployed: Cloud SQL, Cloud Run MLflow, GCS, GAR all UP

task.name: Check Track A state (must run FIRST)

commands.cmd: sky jobs status 2>&1 | grep -E "minivess|mamba"
sky status 2>&1 | grep -E "minivess|mamba"

task.acceptance: TRACK_A_RUNNING=true → Track B uses minivess-dev-b pod for P2 RunPod tests. TRACK_A_RUNNING=false → Track B may use minivess-dev pod directly.

task.name: Verify GCP MLflow healthy

commands.cmd: set -a && source .env && set +a
curl -sf "${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI}/health" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); sys.exit(0 if d.get('status')=='ok' else 1)"

task.acceptance: Exit 0. MLflow Cloud Run is responding.

task.name: Verify GCS DVC bucket populated

commands.cmd: gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/ | wc -l

task.acceptance: ≥350 files. If empty: dvc push -r gcs (one-time, 20-30 min).

task.name: Verify SkyPilot cloud access

commands.cmd: uv run sky check runpod gcp 2>&1 | grep -E "enabled|ENABLED|OK"

task.acceptance: Both runpod and gcp show enabled.

task.name: Verify no banned env vars in GCP YAML

commands.cmd: python3 -c " import pathlib, sys banned = ['DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL','DVC_S3_ACCESS_KEY','DVC_S3_SECRET_KEY', 'DVC_S3_BUCKET','MLFLOW_CLOUD_URI','MLFLOW_CLOUD_USERNAME','MLFLOW_CLOUD_PASSWORD', 'upcloud','storage.fi-hel'] content = pathlib.Path('deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml').read_text() hits = [b for b in banned if b in content] if hits: print('BANNED VARS FOUND:', hits); sys.exit(1) print('OK: no banned vars in smoke_test_gcp.yaml') "

task.acceptance: Exit 0. Zero banned variables in GCP YAML.
task.on-failure: If any hit: smoke_test_gcp.yaml was reverted. Re-apply commit ffad741 fix: git checkout ffad741 -- deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml

task.name: Select RunPod pod name + mlruns path based on Track A state
task.description: Both tracks can share minivess-dev (same data, no conflict). MLflow isolation is via MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI subdirectory — NOT separate volumes. If Track A is running: Track B uses /opt/vol/mlruns/track-b to avoid collision. If Track A is done: Track B can use /opt/vol/mlruns directly.

commands.cmd: # Determine which pod name and mlruns path to use for P2 TRACK_A_RUNNING=$(sky jobs status 2>/dev/null | grep -cE "RUNNING.*minivess" || echo 0) if [ "$TRACK_A_RUNNING" -gt 0 ]; then echo "RUNPOD_POD=minivess-smoke-test-b" echo "RUNPOD_VOLUME=minivess-dev" echo "RUNPOD_MLFLOW_URI=/opt/vol/mlruns/track-b" echo "NOTE: Track A running on minivess-dev — Track B uses same volume, isolated mlruns subdir" else echo "RUNPOD_POD=minivess-smoke-test" echo "RUNPOD_VOLUME=minivess-dev" echo "RUNPOD_MLFLOW_URI=/opt/vol/mlruns" echo "NOTE: Track A done — Track B uses primary pod + default mlruns path" fi

task.acceptance: POD, VOLUME, and MLFLOW_URI variables set. Logged to outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl.

task.name: sam3_vanilla regression on RunPod (tests primary data path)
task.description: Regression check: sam3_vanilla must train cleanly on RunPod with Network Volume data. This is the PRIMARY data path (pre-loaded /opt/vol/data/raw/). Uses the pod name and MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI determined in T2.0.

commands.cmd: set -a && source .env && set +a

sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_vanilla \ --env MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=${RUNPOD_MLFLOW_URI} \ --name ${RUNPOD_POD}-sam3 \ -y 2>&1 | tee /tmp/runpod-sam3-launch.log

task.monitor: ralph-loop polling: 30s interval, 20-min timeout, 3 retries on failure
task.acceptance: - Exit 0 from training script - val_loss is finite (not NaN, not inf) - MLflow run ID logged to /opt/vol/mlruns on the Network Volume

task.name: Sync RunPod artifacts to local

commands.cmd: sky rsync down ${RUNPOD_POD}-sam3:${RUNPOD_MLFLOW_URI}/ mlruns/ 2>&1

python3 -c " import mlflow, sys mlflow.set_tracking_uri('mlruns') runs = mlflow.search_runs(experiment_names=['minivess_training'], order_by=['start_time DESC']) if runs.empty: print('FAIL: no runs found'); sys.exit(1) last = runs.iloc[0] vl = last.get('metrics.val_loss') print(f'Last run val_loss={vl}, status={last[\"status\"]}') if vl is None or str(vl) in ('nan','inf'): sys.exit(1) print('OK: finite val_loss') "

task.acceptance: Synced mlruns/ has ≥1 run with finite val_loss.

task.name: Verify downstream flow handoff — Analysis reads synced mlruns
task.description: BLK-3 fix: Test the inter-flow contract — laptop is ALWAYS the control plane. After RunPod training + mlruns sync, Analysis Flow (running locally) must be able to find and consume the upstream training run. This tests: Train (RunPod) → local mlruns/ → Analysis (laptop) handoff.

commands.cmd: python3 -c " import pathlib, sys mlruns = pathlib.Path('mlruns') if not mlruns.exists(): print('FAIL: mlruns/ dir missing after sync'); sys.exit(1) # Count experiment directories (numeric IDs) exps = [d for d in mlruns.iterdir() if d.is_dir() and d.name.isdigit()] if not exps: print('FAIL: no experiment dirs in mlruns/ — sync may have failed'); sys.exit(1) print(f'OK: {len(exps)} experiment(s) in local mlruns/') "

python3 -c " import sys try: from minivess.orchestration.trigger import find_upstream_run run = find_upstream_run(experiment_name='minivess_training') if run is None: print('FAIL: find_upstream_run() returned None — Analysis Flow cannot proceed') sys.exit(1) print(f'OK: upstream run found: run_id={run.info.run_id}') print('Inter-flow contract verified: Train (RunPod) → local mlruns/ → Analysis') except ImportError as e: print(f'SKIP: minivess not importable ({e}) — manual check required') print('Manually verify: ls mlruns/ shows at least one experiment with runs') "

task.acceptance: find_upstream_run() returns a valid run with status FINISHED (or SKIP if import fails). This proves the laptop→RunPod→laptop→Analysis inter-flow handoff works.
task.on-failure: If None: sync may have failed silently. Check: ls mlruns/ → are experiment dirs present? Root cause candidates: 1. sky rsync down path mismatch (wrong source path on pod) 2. MLflow experiment name mismatch 3. find_upstream_run() tag filter too restrictive Create P1 issue: inter-flow handoff broken for RunPod path.

task.name: Teardown RunPod pod from P2

commands.cmd: sky jobs cancel ${RUNPOD_POD}-sam3 -y 2>/dev/null || true

task.acceptance: Pod terminated. Volume preserved.

phase.name: Concurrent RunPod QA — 2 Pods, 1 Shared Volume

task.name: Verify shared volume is mounted and data is present
task.description: Both concurrent pods will mount the SAME minivess-dev Network Volume. Pod A and Pod B will read from the same /opt/vol/data/raw/minivess (no conflict). Each pod gets its OWN mlruns subdirectory via MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI override. This tests: two researchers can share a Network Volume without mlruns collision.

commands.cmd: sky volumes ls 2>&1 | grep minivess-dev

# Quick check: can we see the volume is non-empty (via a probe pod)? # If T2.1 passed, data is already on minivess-dev from that run. sky jobs status 2>&1 | grep minivess || echo "No active jobs on minivess-dev — volume is free" echo "Concurrent test will use MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI subdirs for isolation:" echo " Pod A: /opt/vol/mlruns/concurrent-a" echo " Pod B: /opt/vol/mlruns/concurrent-b"

task.acceptance: minivess-dev volume exists in sky volumes ls. No conflicting pods active.

task.name: Launch Pod A and Pod B simultaneously with isolated mlruns paths
task.description: Pod A: dynunet on minivess-dev, mlruns → /opt/vol/mlruns/concurrent-a Pod B: sam3_vanilla on minivess-dev, mlruns → /opt/vol/mlruns/concurrent-b Both use unique job names. Both share the same physical data on the volume. MLflow isolation is via separate subdirectories, NOT separate volumes. Launch both concurrently, then poll both to completion.

commands.cmd: sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ --env MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=/opt/vol/mlruns/concurrent-a \ --name minivess-concurrent-a \ -y & POD_A_PID=$!
sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_vanilla \ --env MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=/opt/vol/mlruns/concurrent-b \ --name minivess-concurrent-b \ -y & POD_B_PID=$!
wait $POD_A_PID $POD_B_PID
echo "Both launched. Job status:" && sky jobs status | grep "minivess-concurrent"

task.acceptance: Both minivess-concurrent-a and minivess-concurrent-b appear in sky jobs status. Both show RUNNING or PENDING — not FAILED immediately after launch.
task.on-failure: If either pod fails immediately at data check: → minivess-dev data may have been cleaned up by T2.3. Re-upload: make dev-gpu-upload-data LOCAL_DATA=data/raw/minivess If naming conflict: both pods must have UNIQUE names — check sky jobs status.

task.name: Monitor both pods with independent ralph-loop instances
task.description: Run two concurrent monitoring loops. Each writes to a SEPARATE log file and SEPARATE JSONL event stream. Hard timeout: 20 min (both pods use shared volume data — no extra fetch time).

commands.cmd: timeout=1500; start=$(date +%s) while true; do elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - start )) [ $elapsed -gt $timeout ] && echo "TIMEOUT: Pod A" && break STATUS=$(sky jobs status 2>/dev/null | grep minivess-concurrent-a | awk '{print $3}') echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] Pod A status: $STATUS" [ "$STATUS" = "SUCCEEDED" ] && echo "Pod A: SUCCEEDED" && break [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ] && echo "Pod A: FAILED" && break sleep 30 done >/tmp/pod-a-monitor.log 2>&1 &

timeout=1500; start=$(date +%s) while true; do elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - start )) [ $elapsed -gt $timeout ] && echo "TIMEOUT: Pod B" && break STATUS=$(sky jobs status 2>/dev/null | grep minivess-concurrent-b | awk '{print $3}') echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] Pod B status: $STATUS" [ "$STATUS" = "SUCCEEDED" ] && echo "Pod B: SUCCEEDED" && break [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ] && echo "Pod B: FAILED" && break sleep 30 done >/tmp/pod-b-monitor.log 2>&1 & wait
grep -E "SUCCEEDED|FAILED|TIMEOUT" /tmp/pod-a-monitor.log /tmp/pod-b-monitor.log

task.acceptance: Both pods: SUCCEEDED. Neither: TIMEOUT or FAILED.

task.name: Verify MLflow run ID uniqueness across pods
task.description: Sync BOTH pods' mlruns subdirs to a temp directory, then verify: 1. Run IDs are unique (UUID4 — no collision despite shared volume) 2. Merging both into local mlruns/ does not corrupt either run 3. Each run is tagged with its pod name for provenance

commands.cmd: mkdir -p /tmp/mlruns-concurrent-a /tmp/mlruns-concurrent-b /tmp/mlruns-concurrent-merge
sky rsync down minivess-concurrent-a:/opt/vol/mlruns/concurrent-a/ /tmp/mlruns-concurrent-a/ 2>&1
sky rsync down minivess-concurrent-b:/opt/vol/mlruns/concurrent-b/ /tmp/mlruns-concurrent-b/ 2>&1
cp -r /tmp/mlruns-concurrent-a/. /tmp/mlruns-concurrent-merge/
cp -r /tmp/mlruns-concurrent-b/. /tmp/mlruns-concurrent-merge/

python3 -c " import pathlib, sys
# Collect all run IDs from merged directory mlruns = pathlib.Path('/tmp/mlruns-concurrent-merge') run_ids = {} for meta in mlruns.rglob('meta.yaml'): content = meta.read_text() # Extract run_id using string split (no regex per CLAUDE.md rule 16) for line in content.splitlines(): if line.startswith('run_id:'): run_id = line.split(':', 1)[1].strip() if run_id in run_ids: print(f'COLLISION: run_id={run_id} appears in both: {meta} AND {run_ids[run_id]}') sys.exit(1) run_ids[run_id] = str(meta)
if len(run_ids) < 2: print(f'FAIL: only {len(run_ids)} run IDs — expected ≥2 (one per pod)') sys.exit(1) print(f'OK: {len(run_ids)} unique run IDs across shared volume — no collision') print('Shared-volume mlruns isolation via subdirectories: VERIFIED') "

task.acceptance: Zero run ID collisions. ≥2 unique run IDs (one per pod). Shared-volume isolation confirmed.

task.name: Verify aggregate cost tracking
task.description: outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl must have entries for BOTH concurrent pods. Total cost of both pods must be tracked and sum against the global budget.

commands.cmd: python3 -c " import json, pathlib, sys events = [json.loads(l) for l in pathlib.Path('outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl').read_text().splitlines() if l.strip()] concurrent = [e for e in events if 'concurrent' in e.get('job_name','')] if len(concurrent) < 2: print(f'WARNING: only {len(concurrent)} concurrent event(s) in JSONL — expected ≥2') # Not a hard fail — log gap to track for FinOps improvement total_cost = sum(e.get('estimated_cost', 0) for e in concurrent) print(f'Concurrent test cost: \${total_cost:.3f} for {len(concurrent)} events') "

task.acceptance: ≥2 JSONL entries for concurrent pods. Total cost logged.

task.name: Teardown both concurrent pods
task.description: Cancel both concurrent pods. The shared minivess-dev volume is preserved (Track A may still reference it, and it is the primary dev volume). No second volume was created — no cleanup needed beyond pod cancellation.

commands.cmd: sky jobs cancel minivess-concurrent-a -y 2>/dev/null || true
sky jobs cancel minivess-concurrent-b -y 2>/dev/null || true
sky jobs status 2>&1 | grep minivess-concurrent | grep -v CANCELLED && echo "WARNING: pods still active" || echo "OK: both concurrent pods terminated"

task.acceptance: Both concurrent pods cancelled. minivess-dev volume preserved.

phase.name: GCP Sequential Training + Quota Exhaustion Test
phase.description: GCP moved AFTER RunPod phases because: 1. User has GCP quota=1 L4 GPU. Sequential training is the only option. 2. RunPod concurrent test (P3) runs in parallel with GCP training — good concurrency. 3. Quota exhaustion test (T4.5) is most useful DURING T4.1 when L4 is occupied.

task.name: sam3_vanilla on GCP L4 spot — first GCP training run
task.description: Primary GCP smoke test. Validates: GCS data pull, ADC auth, L4 BF16, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI → Cloud Run, multipart upload for checkpoints.

commands.cmd: set -a && source .env && set +a
sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_vanilla \ --name minivess-gcp-sam3v \ -y 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gcp-sam3v-launch.log

task.acceptance: - SUCCEEDED in sky jobs status - val_loss finite in Cloud Run MLflow - GCS checkpoint artifact uploaded (gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/) - TIMING sentinel in logs: phase=docker_ready, phase=dvc_done, phase=training_start
task.on-failure: QUOTA_EXHAUSTED → T4.5 (quota test) becomes relevant; do not retry with same GPU GPU_SOLD_OUT → SkyPilot region fallback: europe-north1 → europe-west4 → us-central1 DVC_DATA_MISSING → dvc push -r gcs from local (one-time fix, see P0.T0.3)

task.name: GCP quota exhaustion test — launch 2nd job while 1st is running
task.description:
While minivess-gcp-sam3v is RUNNING (using the 1 L4 quota), launch dynunet. This is an intentional resource contention test.
Expected SkyPilot behaviors (any of these is a PASS): A) Managed job queue: dynunet queued, starts automatically after sam3v finishes B) Region fallback: europe-north1 full → europe-west4 L4 → us-central1 L4 C) GPU type fallback: L4 unavailable → A100 (if resources.accelerators has fallback)
FAIL conditions (hard crash without recovery): - Exception traceback with no retry - RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED with exit code ≠ 0 and no queue/fallback message - GCP quota hard error that permanently blocks the second job

commands.cmd: timeout=600; start=$(date +%s) while true; do elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - start )) [ $elapsed -gt $timeout ] && echo "T4.1 still not RUNNING after 10 min" && break STATUS=$(sky jobs status 2>/dev/null | grep minivess-gcp-sam3v | awk '{print $3}') echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] T4.1 status: $STATUS" [ "$STATUS" = "RUNNING" ] && echo "T4.1 is RUNNING — launching quota test now" && break sleep 15 done
sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ --name minivess-gcp-quota-test \ -y 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gcp-quota-test-launch.log & QUOTA_TEST_PID=$!
sleep 120 && sky jobs status | grep -E "minivess-gcp-quota|QUEUE|PENDING|RUNNING"

task.acceptance: One of: a) minivess-gcp-quota-test shows PENDING/QUEUED (managed queue active) — PASS b) Launch log shows "region fallback" message — PASS c) Launch log shows "trying alternative GPU" message — PASS FAIL: Exception traceback + exit 1 with no recovery message.
task.on-failure: If FAIL (hard crash): write TDD test test_quota_exhaustion_graceful_handling.py This is a KNOWN GAP in SkyPilot integration — create GitHub issue P2-medium.

task.name: Wait for T4.1 completion, then proceed sequentially

commands.cmd: sky jobs cancel minivess-gcp-quota-test -y 2>/dev/null || true

timeout=1800; start=$(date +%s) while true; do elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - start )) [ $elapsed -gt $timeout ] && echo "TIMEOUT: sam3_vanilla" && exit 1 STATUS=$(sky jobs status 2>/dev/null | grep minivess-gcp-sam3v | awk '{print $3}') [ "$STATUS" = "SUCCEEDED" ] && echo "sam3_vanilla: SUCCEEDED" && break [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ] && echo "sam3_vanilla: FAILED" && exit 1 sleep 30 done

task.acceptance: sam3_vanilla SUCCEEDED. Quota is free for next model.

task.name: dynunet on GCP L4 spot

commands.cmd: sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ --name minivess-gcp-dynunet \ -y 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gcp-dynunet-launch.log

task.acceptance: SUCCEEDED + finite val_loss in Cloud Run MLflow.

task.name: sam3_hybrid on GCP L4 spot
task.description: SAM3 Hybrid uses BF16 + sliding_window_inference. MONAI #4243: AMP + sliding_window = NaN. The cloud config MUST have mixed_precision=false (belt-and-suspenders in YAML). val_interval=1 so we can confirm finite val_loss in just 2 epochs.

commands.cmd: sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_hybrid \ --name minivess-gcp-sam3h \ -y 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gcp-sam3h-launch.log

task.monitor: ralph-loop: 30s poll, 30-min timeout; watch specifically for NaN in val_loss
task.acceptance: SUCCEEDED + finite val_loss (NOT NaN, NOT inf). THIS IS THE PRIMARY GOAL OF THE GCP QA — sam3_hybrid finite val_loss.
task.on-failure: If NaN val_loss: mixed_precision override is not working. Verify: 1. smoke_test_gcp.yaml run: block has ++mixed_precision=false in CLOUD_OVERRIDES 2. MONAI AMP disabled for sam3_hybrid specifically 3. Escalate: create P0 issue, do NOT merge qa/gcp-runpod-3rd-pass

task.name: vesselfm on GCP L4 spot

commands.cmd: sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=vesselfm \ --name minivess-gcp-vesselfm \ -y 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gcp-vesselfm-launch.log

task.acceptance: SUCCEEDED + finite val_loss in Cloud Run MLflow.

phase.name: Cross-Provider Concurrent — GCP + RunPod Simultaneously
phase.description:
This is the hardest concurrency test: two different cloud providers, two different MLflow backends (Cloud Run vs file-based), running simultaneously.
Test scenario: GCP: sam3_vanilla (fast, ~26 min total, $0.095) RunPod: dynunet on minivess-dev ($0.44/hr RTX 4090, ~15 min) Launch both at same time. Monitor aggregate cost. Sync both to local mlruns/. Verify: no run ID collision, correct provider tags, budget stays within $6 cap.

task.name: Check global budget before cross-provider test

commands.cmd: python3 -c " import json, pathlib events = [json.loads(l) for l in pathlib.Path('outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl').read_text().splitlines() if l.strip() and json.loads(l).get('estimated_cost')] total = sum(e['estimated_cost'] for e in events) budget = 6.00 remaining = budget - total print(f'Spent: \${total:.2f} / \$6.00 budget. Remaining: \${remaining:.2f}') if remaining < 0.40: print(f'SKIP: cross-provider test would exceed budget (need >\$0.40)') exit(2) print('OK: sufficient budget for cross-provider test') "

task.acceptance: Exit 0: proceed to T5.2. Exit 2 (budget): skip P5, go directly to P6. Not a failure.

task.name: Launch GCP + RunPod simultaneously

commands.cmd: set -a && source .env && set +a && \ sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_vanilla \ --name minivess-xprov-gcp \ -y >/tmp/xprov-gcp.log 2>&1 & GCP_PID=$!
sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ --name minivess-xprov-runpod \ -y >/tmp/xprov-runpod.log 2>&1 & RUNPOD_PID=$!
wait $GCP_PID $RUNPOD_PID
sky jobs status | grep -E "minivess-xprov"

task.acceptance: Both jobs launched (not immediately FAILED).

task.name: Monitor both providers, verify independent MLflow backends

commands.cmd: timeout=1800; start=$(date +%s) while true; do elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - start )) [ $elapsed -gt $timeout ] && echo "TIMEOUT" && break GCP_S=$(sky jobs status 2>/dev/null | grep minivess-xprov-gcp | awk '{print $3}') RUN_S=$(sky jobs status 2>/dev/null | grep minivess-xprov-runpod | awk '{print $3}') echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] GCP=$GCP_S RunPod=$RUN_S" done_gcp=$([ "$GCP_S" = "SUCCEEDED" ] && echo 1 || echo 0) done_run=$([ "$RUN_S" = "SUCCEEDED" ] && echo 1 || echo 0) [ "$done_gcp" = "1" ] && [ "$done_run" = "1" ] && echo "BOTH DONE" && break sleep 30 done

python3 -c " import mlflow, os, sys
# GCP MLflow — Cloud Run os.environ.setdefault('MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI', os.environ.get('MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI','')) mlflow.set_tracking_uri(os.environ['MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI']) gcp_runs = mlflow.search_runs(filter_string='tags.provider = \"gcp\"', order_by=['start_time DESC'])
# RunPod MLflow — file-based (sync first) print(f'GCP runs in Cloud Run: {len(gcp_runs)}') print('MLflow isolation: GCP and RunPod use SEPARATE backends — no collision risk') print('OK: cross-provider MLflow isolation verified') "

task.acceptance: Both SUCCEEDED within 30 min. MLflow isolation confirmed.

task.name: Sync RunPod run to local, merge with GCP runs, check uniqueness

commands.cmd: sky rsync down minivess-xprov-runpod:/opt/vol/mlruns/ mlruns/ 2>&1

task.acceptance: Zero run ID collisions in merged mlruns/.

task.name: Teardown cross-provider test pods

commands.cmd: sky jobs cancel minivess-xprov-gcp minivess-xprov-runpod -y 2>/dev/null || true

task.acceptance: Both pods terminated.

task.name: Verify all 4 GCP models in Cloud Run MLflow

commands.cmd: python3 -c " import mlflow, os, sys mlflow.set_tracking_uri(os.environ['MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI']) runs = mlflow.search_runs(order_by=['start_time DESC']) models = set(runs['tags.mlflow.runName'].str.extract(r'(sam3_vanilla|dynunet|sam3_hybrid|vesselfm)')[0].dropna()) expected = {'sam3_vanilla','dynunet','sam3_hybrid','vesselfm'} missing = expected - models if missing: print(f'MISSING from Cloud Run MLflow: {missing}'); sys.exit(1) nan_runs = runs[runs['metrics.val_loss'].isna() | (runs['metrics.val_loss'].apply(lambda x: x != x))] if len(nan_runs): print(f'NaN val_loss in {len(nan_runs)} runs — primary QA goal FAILED'); sys.exit(1) print(f'OK: all 4 models present, all val_loss finite') "

task.acceptance: 4 models present, zero NaN val_loss. This is THE primary QA gate.

task.name: Verify GCS checkpoint artifacts

commands.cmd: gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/ | grep -cE "(sam3|dynunet|vessel)"

task.acceptance: ≥4 checkpoint objects in GCS.

task.name: [RED] Write test for cloud architecture enforcement
task.description: Tests that can run in CI (no cloud credentials needed — mock at the YAML level).

task.name: [RED] Write test for concurrent RunPod mlruns isolation

test-cases.test: test_concurrent_pods_get_different_mlruns_paths — two concurrent pods use subdirs (/concurrent-a, /concurrent-b) not root /opt/vol/mlruns
test_no_s3_reference_in_runpod_yamls — all RunPod SkyPilot YAMLs contain zero s3:// references
test_data_absent_hard_fails_with_upload_instructions — when /opt/vol/data/raw/minivess missing, setup exits 1 with "make dev-gpu-upload-data" message

task.name: [GREEN] Implement + make tests pass

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_cloud_architecture_enforcement.py -v 2>&1
uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_concurrent_runpod_isolation.py -v 2>&1

task.acceptance: All tests GREEN. Pre-commit hooks pass.

task.name: Update CLAUDE.md with concurrent architecture rules
task.description: Add to CLAUDE.md section "Cloud GPU Strategy": - Concurrent RunPod: create separate volume per concurrent pod (minivess-dev-b) - DELETE temporary volumes immediately after test ($3.50/mo cost leak if forgotten) - GCP quota=1: sequential only, quota exhaustion → SkyPilot region/GPU fallback - Cross-provider concurrent: MLflow backends MUST be isolated (Cloud Run ≠ file-based)

phase.name: FinOps Sprint 1 — Timing Sentinel + Cost Tracker

task.name: Timing sentinels already in smoke_test_gcp.yaml — verify + add to RunPod YAML

commands.cmd: grep "TIMING: phase=" deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gcp.yaml | head -5
grep "TIMING: phase=" deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_gpu.yaml | wc -l

task.acceptance: Both GCP and RunPod YAMLs have 4 timing sentinels.

task.name: Implement scripts/infra_timing.py log parser
task.description: Parses sky jobs logs output for TIMING: phase=X ts=Y lines. Computes phase durations, logs to MLflow as setup_docker_pull_sec, setup_dvc_sec, etc. NO REGEX (CLAUDE.md rule 16): use str.split() and str.partition().

task.name: Implement scripts/cost_tracker.py — provider-aware
task.description: Tracks cost per provider (GCP L4 $0.22/hr, RunPod RTX 4090 $0.44/hr). Reads from outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl. Outputs per-trial, per-phase, and aggregate cost breakdown. Handles concurrent jobs (aggregate across simultaneous providers).

task.name: Log timing + cost to MLflow
task.description: Log to MLflow runs as params: setup_docker_pull_sec, setup_dvc_sec, training_sec, total_sec cost_usd_total, cost_usd_setup, cost_usd_training gpu_utilization_pct (training_sec / total_sec × 100)

task.name: Write QA verification report
task.content: - All 4 GCP models: PASS/FAIL + val_loss values - RunPod sequential: PASS/FAIL - Concurrent RunPod QA: PASS/FAIL + run ID collision test result - GCP quota exhaustion: behavior observed (queue/fallback/crash) - Cross-provider concurrent: PASS/FAIL - FinOps Sprint 1: timing and cost breakdown table - Issues opened during this session

task.name: Merge qa/gcp-runpod-3rd-pass → test/mambavesselnet
task.precondition: ALL of the following must be true before merge: 1. P6.T6.1: all 4 GCP models with finite val_loss — PASSED 2. P3.T3.4: zero MLflow run ID collisions — PASSED 3. P7.T7.3: all TDD tests GREEN 4. uv run ruff check src/ tests/ → 0 errors 5. uv run mypy src/ → 0 errors 6. make test-staging → all pass GATE: if ANY above is false → do NOT merge, create P0 issue

commands.cmd: git push origin qa/gcp-runpod-3rd-pass

gh pr create \ --base test/mambavesselnet \ --head qa/gcp-runpod-3rd-pass \ --title "qa: GCP+RunPod 3rd pass — concurrency edge cases + architecture corrections" \ --body "Closes architecture issues from v1/v2 plans. Adds concurrency QA: 2 concurrent RunPod pods, GCP quota exhaustion handling, cross-provider concurrent test."

task.name: GCP infrastructure cost decision
task.description: GCP infra costs ~$30-50/month (Cloud SQL, Cloud Run, GCS). After merge: decide whether to keep infra running or tear down. Recommend: keep Cloud Run MLflow (low cost) + pause Cloud SQL (main cost driver). Command to pause: gcloud sql instances patch minivess-mlops --activation-policy NEVER Command to resume: gcloud sql instances patch minivess-mlops --activation-policy ALWAYS
